title: Buffalo Crime + Permits Tracker
text:
Buffalo open data




A simple 30-day snapshot of public safety activity and permit issuance across Buffalo, combining live incident points, permit points, and neighborhood-level context in one place.




Window


Mar 21, 2026 to Apr 19, 2026




Updated


Apr 19, 2026 11:18 PM EDT








Summary









Crime incidents
698
Reported incidents in the rolling 30-day window.


Permits issued
921
Issued permits in the same reporting window.


Declared permit value
$101,413,225
Total declared value of work across 30-day permits.


Average permits per day
31
Based on a 30-day rolling window.








Main map



Crime incidents are shown first so the map stays readable at city scale. Turn on permit, demolition, or neighborhood layers from the control when you want to compare them.














Neighborhoods



This supporting map keeps the neighborhood view simple and focused instead of splitting attention across multiple smaller maps.

Method








Data is pulled from Buffalo Open Data during render rather than from local snapshots.

Crime Incidents: https://data.buffalony.gov/d/d6g9-xbgu
Permits: https://data.buffalony.gov/d/9p2d-f3yt
Neighborhoods: https://data.buffalony.gov/d/ekfg-mtu8




The reporting window is calculated in America/New_York and includes today plus the previous 29 days. Crime data is preliminary, and both tables depend on upstream schema stability from Buffalo Open Data.

title: About
text:
This Quarto site tracks recent Buffalo crime incidents and permits using live Buffalo Open Data feeds.
The current implementation focuses on the landing page and is designed to render as a static site on GitHub Actions each day.

title: Buffalo Crime + Permits Tracker
text: Crime and permit points are visible by default. Use the layer control to switch between neighborhood crime counts and neighborhood permit counts.

title: Buffalo Crime + Permits Tracker
text: Crime incidents are shown first so the map stays readable at city scale. Turn on permits or neighborhood overlays from the layer control when you want to compare layers.
